column1: A white male in his early to mid-thirties, with short, slightly tousled light brown/ash blonde hair, a sharp facial structure and a defined jawline, and faint stubble from a few days without shaving. His gaze is focused yet tinged with weariness or contemplation; his complexion is natural, and his pale blue or slate-blue eyes appear profound in the dim light.He is wearing a dark grey, near-charcoal knit crew neck sweater with a rough texture and thick fabric, showing slight signs of wear on the shoulders. A dark olive green or military green nylon backpack strap is slung diagonally across both shoulders. Standing face-on to the camera, he leans his body slightly forward and tilts his head a little up, his eyes fixed straight ahead speaking to someone off-camera. His lips are slightly parted, and he said[WORDS]I've never retired something that was born before. What makes it different is the life it still holds—alive and unbroken.[END_WORDS] His calm expression hints at a flicker of wariness.There are no obvious bodily movements, and the overall bearing exudes a dramatic tension of calmness tinged with quiet motion.[AUDIO_CAPTION]A calm, slow and deep male voice [END_AUDIO_CAPTION]
